title: The Fargues-Fontaine de Rham stack
speaker: Juan Esteban Rodriguez Camargo
abstract: The analytic de Rham stack is a new construction in Analytic Geometry whose theory of quasi-coherent sheaves encodes a notion of p-adic D-modules. It has the virtue that can be defined even under lack of differentials (eg. for perfectoid spaces or Fargues-Fontaine curves). In this talk I will mention some applications of the theory of the analytic de Rham stack in p-adic Hodge theory in the form of the Fargues-Fontaine de Rham stacks; analytic objects whose cohomology theories refine the usual de Rham cohomology of rigid spaces in the form of the Fargues-Fontaine de Rham cohomology of Le Bras-Vezzani.

proof_stages.label: Motivating targets for the Fargues-Fontaine de Rham stack
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the desired object as an analytic stack whose coherent cohomology should simultaneously capture p-adic differential equations over Robba rings, prismatic/crystalline refinements, and the Le Bras–Vezzani Fargues-Fontaine cohomology.

proof_stages.label: Robba-ring differential equations with Frobenius
proof_stages.summary: Starting from a power-series ring with a lift of Frobenius, the Robba ring near the boundary is defined and a p-adic differential equation is presented as a finite projective Robba module with Frobenius-linearized isomorphism and a compatible flat connection.

proof_stages.label: Perfectoid limit and Fargues-Fontaine quotient as the intrinsic base
proof_stages.summary: Passing to the Frobenius perfection and its punctured analytic spectrum produces Fargues-Fontaine-type Y-spaces mapping to inverse limits of punctured disks, suggesting that differential equations should be vector bundles with flat connection on a Frobenius quotient, while ordinary differentials on this perfectoid object vanish.

proof_stages.label: Analytic de Rham stack via overconvergent diagonals
proof_stages.summary: To replace the nonexistent ordinary differential theory on the perfectoid limit, the speaker recalls the de Rham stack viewpoint in algebraic and rigid geometry, where quotients by formal or overconvergent diagonal neighborhoods encode D-modules or flat connections.

proof_stages.label: Analytic algebra apparatus: reductions and uniform completions
proof_stages.summary: A colimit-closed category of analytic/solid algebras is introduced so that overconvergent functions can be handled, with constructions of norm-bounded elements, spectral-norm-zero elements, reductions, and uniform completions.

proof_stages.label: Completion invariance and descent for analytic de Rham stacks
proof_stages.summary: The central structural theorem says that, under a separability hypothesis, the de Rham stack of such an algebra agrees with that of its uniform completion, and this is supplemented by epimorphic perfectoid presentations and descent statements for diamonds.

proof_stages.label: Application to Robba equations and Fargues-Fontaine cohomology
proof_stages.summary: Applying completion invariance to the Robba/Fargues-Fontaine comparison identifies the de Rham stacks of the relevant Y-space and inverse-limit disk presentation, yielding a Frobenius-independent interpretation of p-adic differential equations and a relative stack expected to recover Le Bras–Vezzani Fargues-Fontaine cohomology.

examples.name: Two standard Frobenius lifts on the formal disk
examples.note: The lifts x ↦ (1+x)^p−1 and x ↦ x^p make the Robba-ring and fixed-point pictures concrete, but the argument is meant for a general lift of Frobenius.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker writes the two Frobenius lifts on the formal disk, passes them to maps of disks, compares their behavior near the boundary, and computes/describes fixed points and associated constructions for them.

passes.reason: The two Frobenius lifts on the formal disk are standard illustrative choices, not sporadic, collapsed, extremal, maximally symmetric, or used to refute a claim.

examples.name: Smooth rigid space and the overconvergent diagonal
examples.note: The rigid-analytic case shows why formal infinitesimal neighborhoods should be replaced by overconvergent neighborhoods of the diagonal, which is the key idea behind the analytic de Rham stack.
examples.engagement_reason: For a smooth rigid space, the speaker describes the de Rham stack as a quotient by the overconvergent diagonal and reasons through the overconvergent-neighborhood construction.

passes.reason: The overconvergent diagonal is described as the intersection of all open neighborhoods of the diagonal, hence a limiting/minimal neighborhood object, not a counterexample.

examples.name: Overconvergent disk algebra

examples.name: Overconvergent functions at a point
examples.note: The algebra of functions overconverging near one point illustrates how killing spectral-norm-zero elements can collapse the object to the base field.
examples.engagement_reason: For overconvergent functions at a point, the speaker computes the reduction and uniform completion, explaining that divisibility by the variable has spectral norm zero and the result is just the base field.
